qa_pairs.question: What types of IPv6 addresses are available within the Arbutus cloud environment, and how are Global Unicast Addresses (GUAs) set up?
qa_pairs.answer: IPv6 Link-Local (LLA) and Global Unicast Addresses (GUA) are generally available within the Arbutus cloud environment. GUAs are set up using Stateless Address Auto Configuration (SLAAC), which automatically configures the IP on the VM interface.

qa_pairs.question: In the Arbutus cloud, what are the default security rules for IPv6 Global Unicast Addresses (GUAs) on a virtual machine (VM)?
qa_pairs.answer: By default, security group rules will allow all outbound traffic from the VM via the IPv6 GUA, but no traffic originating from outside the VM will be allowed until specific security group rules have been defined. This behaviour is consistent with IPv4.

qa_pairs.question: How does one attach a new IPv6 network interface to an existing virtual machine (VM) in the Arbutus cloud using the OpenStack command-line interface?
qa_pairs.answer: First, retrieve the ID of the VM using `openstack server list`. Then, assign a new network interface to the VM by executing the command `openstack server add network <VM_ID> IPv6-GUA`, where `<VM_ID>` is the identifier of the target virtual machine.
